node_query.reasoning: The hypothesis explicitly targets “6-connected metal nodes” and lists the metal set “Cu, Ni, Co, Mg, Zn”, emphasizing these as OMS-capable after activation. The binding mode is stated as “carboxylate-based frameworks” / “dicarboxylate linkers”, which implies O-donor coordination to the node (PorMake: route donor atom to node.ligand_chemistry). Open metal sites are explicitly required via building_block_properties.has_open_metal_site=true.

linker_query.reasoning: The linker is specified as “Ditopic linkers only” with “light, rigid, aromatic dicarboxylates” and a “benzene dicarboxylate-like and naphthalene dicarboxylate-like” backbone preference. Rigid is supported by “rigid, aromatic”. Length is explicitly given as “approx. 5–12 Å”. Carboxylate is a coordination group and therefore is not placed in linker tags/functional_groups; instead it is captured as O-donor chemistry in node_query.ligand_chemistry.
